name: VERTEX PHARMACEUTICALS INC / MA
business: Vertex Pharmaceuticals develops and commercializes medicines for cystic fibrosis, sickle cell disease, transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, and acute pain. Its products include TRIKAFTA, ALYFTREK, CASGEVY, and JOURNAVX. The company sells primarily to specialty pharmacies, distributors, wholesalers, hospitals, clinics, and government entities in the U.S. and international markets.

exposures.sentence: Our five CF medicines, led by TRIKAFTA/KAFTRIO, are being used to treat nearly three quarters of the people with CF in the U.S., Europe, Australia, and Canada.

CASGEVY, our ex-vivo, non-viral CRISPR/Cas9 gene-edited cell therapy, is approved in the U.S., the E.U., the U.K., the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Kingdom of Bahrain, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, Switzerland and Canada for the treatment of people 12 years of age and older with SCD or TDT.
JOURNAVX, our selective non-opioid NaV1.8 pain signal inhibitor, is approved in the U.S. for the treatment of people with moderate-to-severe acute pain.
We are developing povetacicept, a dual inhibitor of B cell activating factor (BAFF) and a proliferation-inducing ligand (APRIL) cytokines, for multiple diseases.
Inaxaplin is our small molecule for the treatment of APOL1-mediated kidney disease (AMKD).
Zimislecel is an allogeneic, stem cell-derived, fully differentiated, insulin-producing islet cell replacement therapy, using standard immunosuppression to protect the implanted cells.

We previously initiated the first Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating suzetrigine for the treatment of people with diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN), a common form of peripheral neuropathic pain, and have initiated a second Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating suzetrigine in DPN in the fourth quarter of 2025.
In addition, we face certain risks as we seek to leverage artificial intelligence to optimize productivity and efficiency in various aspects of the organization.
Cyber-attacks and incidents are increasing in their frequency, sophistication, and intensity, and are difficult to detect.

Disruption in our supply chain or manufacturing capabilities can result in shipment delays, inventory shortages, lot failures, product withdrawals, recalls and other interruptions in the commercial and clinical supply of our products and product candidates.
For example, we depend on China-based suppliers for portions of our supply chain.

catalysts.event: Begin global regulatory submissions for CASGEVY in children 5 to 11 years of age
catalysts.sentence: We expect to begin global regulatory submissions for approvals for CASGEVY in children 5 to 11 years of age, in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Submit for approval of ALYFTREK in children 2 to 5 years of age
catalysts.sentence: Following positive results from this clinical trial, we expect to submit for approval with global regulators in this age group in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Complete rolling BLA submission for povetacicept in IgA nephropathy
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete the submission in the first half of 2026, if data from the interim analysis are supportive.

catalysts.event: Share interim analysis data for inaxaplin in APOL1-mediated kidney disease
catalysts.sentence: We expect to share data from the interim analysis in late 2026 or early 2027.

catalysts.event: Complete enrollment in both Phase 3 clinical trials of suzetrigine in diabetic peripheral neuropathy
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete enrollment in both Phase 3 clinical trials by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: Complete Phase 2 portion and initiate Phase 3 portion of povetacicept trial in primary membranous nephropathy
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete the Phase 2 portion of the clinical trial and to initiate the Phase 3 portion in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Secured reimbursed access to CASGEVY for SCD in Scotland
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we secured reimbursed access to CASGEVY for eligible people with SCD in Scotland, consistent with the reimbursement agreement reached in 2025 for people with TDT.
